About Me

Jeewoong Kim is a Ph.D candidate who is researching Software Engineer at SDEV lab with a his advisor Shin Hong in Chungbuk National University (CBNU).

Previously, Jeewoong received his M.S degree from Computer Science and Electrical Engineering in Handong Global University.

📧:jeewoong4@gmail.com

Research Interest

Jeewoong researches dynamic program analysis for software verification and debugging.
In particular, he worked on continuous fuzzing for multi-fault environments and techniques for detecting execution-order-dependent bugs.
During his master’s program, he developed a new technique, DoTeLink, to improve unit test quality by automatically linking API documentation to test code that representing the same requirement.

Publications

  • Path-target Directed Greybox Fuzzing with Recursive Search Strategy, KCC, Jul 2025
  • BugOss: A Benchmark of Real-world Regression Bugs for Empirical Investigation of Regression Fuzzing Techniques, JSS, Oct 2024 [git] (presented at the Journal-first track in ASE 2024)
  • BugOss: A Regression Bug Benchmark for Empirical Study of Regression Fuzzing Techniques, ICST-Poster, April 2023
  • Inferring Fine-grained Traceability Links between Javadoc Comment and JUnit Test Code, ICSME-NIER, Oct 2022 [video(kor)]
  • How Does a Unit-level Test Case for Continuous Fuzzing Eolve: An Empirical Study of Code Changes in OSS-Fuzz Projects, KCC, Jun 2022 [pdf] [data]
